reasoning:
The value of WLRRAL for the 2026-02-15 baseline corresponds to the Wednesday, Feb 11 data, which our FRED query identifies as 320,655 million. Over the last two months, non-end-of-month baseline values have oscillated between roughly 317k and 332k, making 320,655 a near-trough print. 
For Date 1 (2026-02-22), the relevant data will be from Wed, Feb 18. There are two strong drivers for an increase: 1) mean reversion from the low 320k print toward the ~326k average, and 2) GSE principal and interest payments, which typically settle on the 15th (or the following business day if a weekend, as on Feb 15). These payments often cause mid-month spikes in ON RRP usage (e.g., Feb 19, 2025 spiked to 461k). Therefore, a strong probability (0.67) is assigned for a jump above 320,655.
For Date 2 (2026-03-17), the relevant data will be from Wed, March 11. This date comfortably misses mid-month GSE payments and end-of-quarter spikes. By then, the underlying structural decay (from quantitative tightening) could further lower the baseline range to ~315k-320k. I assign roughly even odds (0.50), reflecting increased uncertainty as the forecast horizon extends and standard un-inflated seasonal effects balance the downward trend.
